text: NVIDIA demonstrated robust revenue expansion throughout fiscal 2025, with quarterly revenue progressing from $44,062,000,000 in Q1 to $68,127,000,000 in Q4 2026 [0f02741a-d0ea-4bca-99a1-43ba2bf309de][8673c861-f8b6-4f0f-8801-373630314eeb]. Year-over-year growth rates accelerated from 6.08% in Q2 to 21.96% in Q3, before moderating slightly to 19.51% in Q4 [0e671c19-4b44-47a7-9234-5d3634f7be27]. This sequential growth trajectory indicates sustained demand momentum across the company's product portfolio, particularly in data center and AI-related segments.

issues.explanation: The paragraph states 'fiscal 2025' but references 'Q4 2026' and the verified facts confirm the fiscal year ends January 25, 2026 (fiscal 2026). This is a direct contradiction with the previously verified facts about NVIDIA's fiscal year 2026.
issues.recommended_fix: Change 'fiscal 2025' to 'fiscal 2026' to align with the verified company information and the Q4 2026 reference in the same sentence.

issues.offending_span: particularly in data center and AI-related segments
issues.explanation: The cited chunks (0f02741a-d0ea-4bca-99a1-43ba2bf309de and 8673c861-f8b6-4f0f-8801-373630314eeb) only contain overall revenue figures. There is no segment-level breakdown or mention of data center/AI performance in the cited evidence to support this specific claim.
issues.recommended_fix: Remove the segment-specific claim or cite additional evidence chunks that contain segment-level revenue data for data center and AI products.
